name: Glassmorphism
description: A style built on translucent, frosted-glass surfaces using background blur, layered transparency, subtle borders, and soft light effects to create depth and a sense of floating panels over colourful or photographic backgrounds.
emotional_impression: Modern, premium, light, and atmospheric, with a sense of depth and softness.
visual_principles: Subtle 1px light borders to define glass edges
Layered depth through stacked translucent surfaces
Soft vivid background gradients or imagery shining through
Gentle inner and outer glows rather than hard shadows
accessibility_risks: Text over translucent blurred backgrounds frequently fails WCAG contrast because the background luminance varies
Contrast is non-deterministic since it depends on whatever sits behind the glass
Low-vision and bright-sunlight users struggle with low-opacity surfaces
performance_risks: backdrop-filter blur is GPU-expensive and can cause scroll jank on low-end devices
Stacked blurs compound cost and drain battery on mobile
appropriate_contexts: Showcase surfaces with controlled backgrounds where contrast can be guaranteed
OS-style overlays where the platform manages legibility scrims
Hero or marketing moments where atmosphere matters more than dense reading
inappropriate_contexts: Text-heavy or data-dense screens where legibility is paramount
Any surface where the background behind glass is uncontrolled user content
evidence: Popularised by Apple's macOS Big Sur and iOS frosted panels and Microsoft Fluent Acrylic
Nielsen Norman Group and accessibility audits repeatedly flag translucent-over-image text as a top contrast-failure pattern
Heavy template reuse since 2020-2021 makes it a strong AI/generated-look cliche
